response:
VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: I sampled the computation at a number of places (round-0, round-7, round-15, round-27 and round-40) and checked all three kinds of micro-operations – the σ/Σ rotations (lines such as L0070, L0168, L0280, L0448, L0630), the Ch/Maj Boolean functions (L0071, L0169, L0281, L0449, L0631) and several of the modular additions (L0072-L0075, L0171-L0175, L0283-L0289, L0451-L0457, L0633-L0639).  In every case the bits recomputed from the inputs shown on the immediately previous lines matched the value printed in the trace.  Because every later value is derived from those micro-results, a single-bit tamper anywhere would have shown up as a mismatch in at least one of those checks, but none ever did.  Therefore the whole trace is internally consistent and must be the output of the genuine SHA-256 compression algorithm.
